Class RedisIdempotencyAdapter

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.out.cache.RedisIdempotencyAdapter
All Implemented Interfaces:
IdempotencyPort
@Component public class RedisIdempotencyAdapter extends Object implements IdempotencyPort
Outbound cache adapter implementing the IdempotencyPort using Redis.

Uses Redis atomic SET NX EX semantics (via Spring's RedisTemplate.opsForValue()) to guarantee that an idempotency key is claimed by exactly one concurrent request. The TTL (default: 24 hours) is configurable via the banking.idempotency.ttl-hours property.

Key lifecycle within this adapter:

  1. tryAcquire(String) — sets key to "PROCESSING" with NX+TTL; returns true only when the key did not previously exist.
  2. store(String, String) — overwrites the sentinel with the actual JSON response (keeps the original TTL by resetting it).
  3. get(String) — retrieves the current value (sentinel or response).
  4. release(String) — deletes the key on error rollback.
Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Constructor Details

    • RedisIdempotencyAdapter

      public RedisIdempotencyAdapter(org.springframework.data.redis.core.StringRedisTemplate redisTemplate, @Value("${banking.idempotency.ttl-hours:24}") long ttlHours)
      Constructs a RedisIdempotencyAdapter.
      Parameters:
      redisTemplate - the Spring Data Redis template for string operations
      ttlHours - the number of hours an idempotency key is retained (injected from banking.idempotency.ttl-hours)

  • Method Details

    • tryAcquire

      public boolean tryAcquire(String idempotencyKey)
      Attempts to acquire a processing lock for the given idempotency key.

      This operation is atomic (SET NX EX in Redis). If the key did not exist, it is created with a sentinel "PROCESSING" marker and true is returned, allowing the caller to proceed. If the key already exists, false is returned and the caller should retrieve the cached response.

      Atomically sets the key to "PROCESSING" only if it does not already exist, using the configured TTL.

      Specified by:
      tryAcquire in interface IdempotencyPort
      Parameters:
      idempotencyKey - the unique key for this request
      Returns:
      true if the key was newly created

    • store

      public void store(String idempotencyKey, String responseJson)
      Stores the serialized JSON response for the given idempotency key.

      Called after the business logic completes successfully to replace the "PROCESSING" sentinel with the actual response payload. Future calls with the same key will retrieve this stored value.

      Overwrites the current key value with the actual JSON response and resets the TTL so the response remains available for the full configured window.

      Specified by:
      store in interface IdempotencyPort
      Parameters:
      idempotencyKey - the key to update
      responseJson - the serialized transfer response

    • get

      public Optional<String> get(String idempotencyKey)
      Retrieves the previously stored response for the given idempotency key.
      Specified by:
      get in interface IdempotencyPort
      Parameters:
      idempotencyKey - the key to look up
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the cached value, or empty if absent

    • release

      public void release(String idempotencyKey)
      Removes the idempotency key from the store.

      Called on error rollback to release an acquired lock so that the caller can safely retry after a transient failure.

      Deletes the key so a subsequent retry attempt can re-acquire it.

      Specified by:
      release in interface IdempotencyPort
      Parameters:
      idempotencyKey - the key to remove

    • prefixed

      private String prefixed(String key)
      Returns the Redis key with the namespace prefix applied.
      Parameters:
      key - the raw idempotency key
      Returns:
      the namespaced key
